Name: CurvatureAwarePrecisionRouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Neural Network Training
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can dynamically adjust numerical precision during training to optimize both computational efficiency and numerical stability, leading to faster convergence and higher accuracy.
Related Work: Existing work in mixed-precision training (e.g., Mix-GEMM, QAOF) focuses on fixed precision schemes or heuristic-based precision switching. Recent research in curvature-aware methods (e.g., Curvature-aware dynamic precision for PINNs) demonstrates the importance of curvature in numerical stability but lacks a comprehensive framework for precision routing. Our proposal builds on these ideas by integrating curvature diagnostics with precision routing to provide a theoretically grounded and empirically validated solution.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing (CAPR) framework for neural network training. CAPR dynamically adjusts numerical precision based on curvature diagnostics, ensuring numerical stability while optimizing computational efficiency. By leveraging spectral quantization theory and block-level precision tiering, CAPR replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Empirical evaluations on standard benchmarks demonstrate that CAPR achieves competitive predictive performance while providing transparent routing traces and reducing computational overhead. This approach bridges foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints, ensuring resource efficiency and mathematical transparency.
Experiments: Train neural networks on standard benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) using CAPR and compare against fixed-precision and heuristic-based precision switching methods.
Measure computational efficiency (e.g., training time, memory usage) and numerical stability (e.g., gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning) metrics.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of curvature diagnostics and precision routing on training dynamics.
Evaluate the generalization performance using PAC-Bayes bounds and multi-seed variance quantification.
Perform diagnostic loops to quantify feature entanglement and gradient alignment before and after precision switching.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of curvature diagnostics may introduce additional computational overhead, which needs to be carefully managed.
The effectiveness of CAPR may vary across different network architectures and datasets, requiring thorough empirical validation.
The theoretical assumptions underlying PAC-Bayes bounds and stability radii may not always hold in practice, necessitating empirical verification.
